_comment: Cumulative geological CO2 storage baseline for the dashboard's storage-delivered view (planned view 2c), sibling to reference-baseline.json. Reconciles TWO sources that measure DIFFERENT things and must NOT be blended into one headline: (A) Global CCS Institute — PURPOSE-based project count, capacity-leaning; (B) Imperial College London 'London Register of Subsurface CO2 Storage' — MEASURED actual tonnes injected. The 'resolution' of the apparent discrepancy is the documented bridge_waterfall + the per-project bridge table below, NOT a single reconciled number. Design decisions signed off 2026-07-13 (3-way taxonomy; two series + bridge). Populated for REVIEW — see known_gaps before wiring into build_dashboard.py.

gccsi_dedicated.title: Global CCS Institute — The Safety and Permanence of CO2 Geological Storage (Perspective)
gccsi_dedicated.basis: purpose-based project count; cumulative is capacity-leaning / operator-reported
gccsi_dedicated.companion_project_list: Seyyedi, Consoli, Minervini, Hatta & Williams (2024), The Global Landscape of CO2 Storage Projects, SSRN 5063681 (auth-walled; full 18-project enumeration not yet extracted — see known_gaps)

imperial_register.title: Imperial College London — The London Register of Subsurface CO2 Storage (Krevor / Jackson / Zhang)
imperial_register.repo: ImperialCollegeLondon/The-London-Register-of-Subsurface-CO2-Storage (site config only; raw dataset not published in-repo)
imperial_register.basis: measured ACTUAL tonnes injected, verified against operator/regulator reporting

foundational_paper.cite: Zhang, Krevor & Jackson (2022), Environmental Science & Technology Letters 9(8):693-698

taxonomy.note: User approved a 3-way split on 2026-07-13. During extraction the classifying rule was sharpened to be DESTINATION-based (defensible and matches GCCSI's own 18-project set), NOT source-based. Flagged for confirmation: this places Sleipner/Snohvit/Gorgon as 'dedicated' (their CO2 comes from gas processing but is injected into a dedicated saline formation, not a producing reservoir), and leaves only Santos Pre-Salt + Qatar in 'associated'. The co2_source flag on each project preserves the alternative source-based grouping so the rule can be toggled without re-tagging.

classes.dedicated: CO2 injected into a non-producing formation (saline aquifer or depleted field) primarily to store it. == GCCSI's 'dedicated' set.
classes.associated: CO2 reinjected into a producing hydrocarbon reservoir (pressure maintenance / co-mingled with production), e.g. pre-salt gas reinjection.
classes.eor: CO2 injected into oil fields to enhance recovery. Excluded from GCCSI's dedicated count.

assumptions.delivery_factor_note: Imperial's measured finding that reported CAPTURE CAPACITY overstates ACTUAL storage. Zhang 2022: aggregate storage was 19-30% below nameplate capacity (2019: 27-31 Mt actual vs 36 Mt capacity ~= 75-86%; headline 81%). Apply to GCCSI's capacity-leaning cumulative to approximate actual. Treated like fx_rates / status-weighting: a stated, editable assumption.
assumptions.vintage_caveat: The two headline numbers are ~5 years and one measurement-basis apart (GCCSI Jun-2025 capacity vs Imperial 2020/2023 actual). The bridge does NOT hide this; the gap is largely real-world growth + new projects, not error.

imperial_all_storage.includes: ALL geological storage: dedicated + associated + EOR
imperial_all_storage.note: The widely-cited '383 Mt stored since 1996' is THIS number (Imperial, all-storage, actual) — NOT a GCCSI figure and NOT dedicated-only. 2023 flow dominated by EOR/associated: Santos Basin Pre-Salt ~13.0 Mt + Seminole San Andres (US EOR) ~3.9 Mt = >1/3 of the year.

imperial_dedicated_derived.method: Sum of destination-'dedicated' rows in Zhang 2022 Table 1 (Sleipner 18.5 + Snohvit 6.5 + Quest 4.8 + Illinois 1.04 + Aquistore 0.27 + Gorgon ~2 ramping).
imperial_dedicated_derived.confidence: medium — Gorgon 2020 value approximate; needs the register's per-project actual series to firm up (known_gaps).

bridge_waterfall.note: Anchored on Imperial MEASURED actuals to 2020 (the most complete verified per-project set), then reconciled forward to the GCCSI Jun-2025 capacity figure. Every step is a documented adjustment, not a fudge.

steps.label: Imperial ALL storage, actual, cumulative to 2020
less EOR projects (Weyburn/Great Plains, Century, Shute Creek, Gulf Coast, Core Energy, Coffeyville, Arkalon, ACTL, CNPC Jilin, Sinopec, Karamay)
less associated reinjection (Santos Pre-Salt 21.4 + Qatar ~20*)
steps.confidence: low — Qatar value unverified, see known_gaps

steps.label: + 5 years growth & new projects to Jun-2025 (Gorgon ramp-up, Moomba, Northern Lights, Ravenna, new US saline, etc.)
apply delivery factor 0.70-0.81 to approximate GCCSI figure on an ACTUAL basis
steps.note: residual after this vs the growth-adjusted Imperial figure = the irreducible uncertainty band

projects.name: Sleipner
projects.caveat: Equinor acknowledged (Oct 2024) over-reporting captured volumes for a period.

projects.name: Northern Lights (Phase 1)
projects.source: Equinor 2025-08-25 (first CO2 stored); world-first merchant T&S

projects.name: Boundary Dam -> Aquistore (dedicated fraction)
projects.caveat: Most Boundary Dam CO2 goes to Weyburn EOR (counted under EOR); only the Aquistore fraction is dedicated saline storage.

projects.name: Illinois Industrial CCS (ADM Decatur)
projects.source: Zhang 2022 Table 1 (1.042-1.55 across sources); excludes the earlier IBDP demo

projects.name: Gorgon
projects.caveat: Has run well below its ~4 Mtpa nameplate (injection/water-handling constraints).

projects.name: Orca / CarbFix (Iceland dedicated site)
projects.source: GCCSI 2025 country count (Iceland=1); exact project TBC — known_gaps

projects.name: Santos Basin Pre-Salt
projects.source: Zhang 2022 Table 1; Imperial 2025 (13.0 Mt in 2023 — single largest annual)
projects.caveat: GCCSI would not count this as dedicated; largest swing item in the taxonomy choice.

projects.name: Qatar LNG (Ras Laffan)
projects.source: Zhang 2022 Table 1 row appears mis-aligned in extraction (rate>capacity; 63.6 implausible for the period) — VALUE UNVERIFIED, see known_gaps

projects.name: Century -> Hobbs/Denver Unit
projects.source: Zhang 2022 Table 1 (Hobbs 10.78; Denver Unit 16.16; Oxy aggregate 25.66 across linked sinks)

projects.name: Shute Creek
projects.source: Zhang 2022 Table 1 (Chevron/ExxonMobil; large capacity but much CO2 vented/EOR)

known_gaps: GCCSI full 18-project enumeration not extracted — companion paper Seyyedi et al. 2024 (SSRN 5063681) is auth-walled (HTTP 403). Country counts are known; the specific US(6)/China(1)/NZ(1)/Iceland(1) members are partly inferred. Needs the CO2CRC GCCSI library copy or SSRN access.
Qatar LNG (Ras Laffan) cumulative — Zhang 2022 row mis-aligned in PDF extraction (capture rate > capacity; 63.6 Mt implausible for 2010-2020 at 2.1 Mtpa). Verify against the paper's SI table.

Imperial per-project ACTUAL series for 2021-2024 not obtained — the London Register's raw dataset is not in its public GitHub repo (site config .toml only). Only 2023 aggregates + Santos/Seminole are public. Needed to firm up imperial_dedicated_derived beyond 2020.
Gorgon 2020 measured value is approximate (project started 2019, ramping).
post-2020 dedicated projects (Moomba, Northern Lights, Ravenna, new US saline) have reported/capacity figures only — no independent measured-actual series yet.
caveat: Two sources, two different questions. GCCSI answers 'how many dedicated projects and which ones' (purpose-based, forward-looking pipeline). Imperial answers 'how much CO2 actually went underground' (measured, but includes EOR). Neither publishes a clean 'dedicated non-EOR ACTUAL cumulative' headline; the best current answer is GCCSI's >80 Mt across 18 projects, read as a capacity-leaning figure that is plausibly ~20-30% above true injected volume. Present as TWO labelled series joined by the bridge_waterfall — never a single blended number.
